name: GBTW Monitor-Aware Tasks-In-Progress Gate
goal: Stop the GBTW hook blocking a worker with 'TASKS STILL IN PROGRESS' when it is legitimately waiting on a persistent Monitor, by adding a _gbtw_recent_waiting_tool helper (waiting tools only) and wiring it into the hard tasks-in-progress gate (classifier OR helper). Gate-only: the continuation check is left untouched. Deploy is an operator step.

tasks.name: RED — waiting-tool test harness + fixtures
tasks.description: TDD RED. Create worker/hooks/tests/test-gbtw-waiting.sh (sources the hook with GBTW_TEST_MODE=1, calls _gbtw_recent_waiting_tool against fixtures, asserts trimmed stdout 'true'/'') and 8 fixtures w1-w8: w1 monitor-last-turn→true, w2 monitor-within-window→true, w3 monitor-outside-window→'', w4 no-waiting-tool→'', w5 schedulewakeup→true, w6 bash-bg (run_in_background)→'' (regression witness: the helper must NOT match run_in_background — that is the completion-aware classifier's job), w7 askuserquestion→true, w8 malformed-line→true. Run → RED (helper undefined; expect-true cases fail). Stage.

steps.description: Create the 8 w*.jsonl fixtures (exact content in the markdown plan)
Create worker/hooks/tests/test-gbtw-waiting.sh (exact content in the markdown plan)
Run — expect RED (helper undefined → stub returns empty; w6 expects empty — run_in_background is the classifier's job, not the helper's)
steps.expected: results: 3 pass, 5 fail (w1,w2,w5,w7,w8 fail; w3,w4,w6 pass); nonzero exit

tasks.name: GREEN — add the _gbtw_recent_waiting_tool helper
tasks.description: Add _gbtw_recent_waiting_tool to worker/hooks/get-back-to-work-claude.sh immediately after _gbtw_extract_in_flight and BEFORE the GBTW_TEST_MODE early-return (so it is exposed when sourced in test mode). Detects ONLY the waiting tools Monitor/TaskOutput/ScheduleWakeup/AskUserQuestion in the last 3 assistant turns — NOT run_in_background (that is covered completion-aware by _gbtw_extract_in_flight; matching it here would be completion-blind). Prints 'true' when found, empty otherwise; fail-safe empty on any error. Run test-gbtw-waiting (8 pass) and test-gbtw-inflight (10 pass, unchanged) and bash -n. Stage.

steps.description: Add the helper (exact snippet in the markdown plan) after _gbtw_extract_in_flight, before the GBTW_TEST_MODE shim

tasks.name: WIRE — waiting-tool suppression into the hard gate (gate-only)
tasks.description: No unit test for the wiring: the gate approve-path runs in the hook main body (not a function), unreachable via GBTW_TEST_MODE without refactoring the gate into a function (out of scope, hold); no full-hook integration harness exists. The behavior-bearing unit (_gbtw_recent_waiting_tool) is fully unit-tested in Task 2; wiring verified by bash -n + call-site grep + both suites green. GATE-ONLY: in the IN_PROGRESS_OR_PENDING_COUNT!=0 branch, after the _gbtw_extract_in_flight suppression and before increment_block_counter/block_stop, add a waiting-tool approve+exit (gate = classifier OR helper). The continuation-suppression block (_BG_RECENT_REQ_IDS) and the holding/waiting text matcher are LEFT UNTOUCHED — the continuation check already handles Monitor and is not part of this bug. Deploy is an operator step (not a plan task).

steps.description: Add the waiting-tool approve block to the tasks-in-progress gate (after the _gbtw_extract_in_flight block, before block_stop). Do NOT modify the continuation-suppression block or the text matcher.
Helper called once — the gate (comment-proof pattern)
Continuation block left intact — assignment line unique (bare name appears on 3 lines)
